tweets.text:
Jeremy Giffon on why the best investment ideas are simple, and the legendary Richard Rainwater's "one-pager" test:

 "A lot of investors are in the feel clever, look smart game more than the money game.

You either have to say, i'm looking for ideas so complex that no one is going to do them, or it should actually be quite simple.

I'm a bigger believer in simplicity, which is you want to be long Elon.

Or you should just buy big companies when they're at their 200-week moving average. 

I love that idea because it's just so simple. But it's right. The gift is being able to sell that idea. 

One story that I absolutely love on cutting through the mess and getting to complete clarity on an investment is from Richard Rainwater.

You would come into his office with a yellow legal pad, and you would write out your thesis on one page, and then you would tell him what percentage of your net worth you're gonna put in the deal. 

Based on your one-page thesis and the percentage of net worth, he would say yes or no."
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
My second conversation with @jeremygiffon. His first episode became one of the most popular we've ever done.

Since then he's become a friend I talk to every day, so this is a taste of one of those conversations.

We discuss:
- The billion dollar PDF
- Why billionaires have https://t.co/oM4FJpy1hH

tweets.text:
Only a few trading protocols will survive this bear market.

I'm making a bet on 10 of them most likely to do so.

My personal list ↓

thread_tweets.full_text:
Only a few trading protocols will survive this bear market.

I'm making a bet on 10 of them most likely to do so.

My personal list ↓

thread_tweets.text:
1. @HyperliquidX

→ Dominates onchain perps
→ Serious revenue
→ Routes most revenue into HYPE buybacks
→ No VC allocation or messy unlock overhang

Rare case where the token is tightly linked to the business.

Easiest #1 for me.
thread_tweets.full_text:
1. @HyperliquidX

→ Dominates onchain perps
→ Serious revenue
→ Routes most revenue into HYPE buybacks
→ No VC allocation or messy unlock overhang

Rare case where the token is tightly linked to the business.

Easiest #1 for me.

thread_tweets.text:
2. @Uniswap

→ Deepest liquidity and integrations
→ v4 lets builders create custom AMMs, fees, and trading logic
→ If the switch/burn path keeps progressing, UNI gets a more direct link to protocol usage.

Is and always will be the main onchain hub for spot trading.
thread_tweets.full_text:
2. @Uniswap

→ Deepest liquidity and integrations
→ v4 lets builders create custom AMMs, fees, and trading logic
→ If the switch/burn path keeps progressing, UNI gets a more direct link to protocol usage.

Is and always will be the main onchain hub for spot trading.

thread_tweets.text:
3. @coinbase

→ Base gives Coinbase its own onchain venue
→ DEX trading is being pushed inside the Coinbase app
→ Custody/wallets/perps/stablecoins/institutions sit under one roof

The only exchange that captures value whether trading stays on the CEX or moves onchain.
thread_tweets.full_text:
3. @coinbase

→ Base gives Coinbase its own onchain venue
→ DEX trading is being pushed inside the Coinbase app
→ Custody/wallets/perps/stablecoins/institutions sit under one roof

The only exchange that captures value whether trading stays on the CEX or moves onchain.

thread_tweets.text:
4. @JupiterExchange

→ Leading Solana DEX
→ Mobile app pulls in retail flow
→ New Solana venues = more JUP distribution

It's a great bet, but it's still heavily dependent on Solana and how relevant it stays in the future.
thread_tweets.full_text:
4. @JupiterExchange

→ Leading Solana DEX
→ Mobile app pulls in retail flow
→ New Solana venues = more JUP distribution

It's a great bet, but it's still heavily dependent on Solana and how relevant it stays in the future.

thread_tweets.text:
5. @pendle_fi

→ Stablecoins/LSTs/RWAs/points all create new yield markets
→ Becomes more useful as DeFi gets more yield assets

No credible competitor at scale, and DeFi keeps producing more yield to trade. Pendle wins as the category grows.

thread_tweets.text:
8. @arcus_xyz

→ Big distribution edge with Robinhood's 20M+ users
→ Stock perps expand the market past BTC/ETH/SOL leverage
→ Pre-IPO + tokenized assets put it closer to a global trading venue

This is dYdX's giga-brain attempt to stand up to giants that stole its market share. A very smart move if you ask me.

tweets.full_text:
Only a few trading protocols will survive this bear market.

I'm making a bet on 10 of them most likely to do so.

My personal list ↓

1. @HyperliquidX

→ Dominates onchain perps
→ Serious revenue
→ Routes most revenue into HYPE buybacks
→ No VC allocation or messy unlock overhang

Rare case where the token is tightly linked to the business.

Easiest #1 for me.

2. @Uniswap

→ Deepest liquidity and integrations
→ v4 lets builders create custom AMMs, fees, and trading logic
→ If the switch/burn path keeps progressing, UNI gets a more direct link to protocol usage.

Is and always will be the main onchain hub for spot trading.

3. @coinbase

→ Base gives Coinbase its own onchain venue
→ DEX trading is being pushed inside the Coinbase app
→ Custody/wallets/perps/stablecoins/institutions sit under one roof

The only exchange that captures value whether trading stays on the CEX or moves onchain.

4. @JupiterExchange

→ Leading Solana DEX
→ Mobile app pulls in retail flow
→ New Solana venues = more JUP distribution

It's a great bet, but it's still heavily dependent on Solana and how relevant it stays in the future.

5. @pendle_fi

→ Stablecoins/LSTs/RWAs/points all create new yield markets
→ Becomes more useful as DeFi gets more yield assets

No credible competitor at scale, and DeFi keeps producing more yield to trade. Pendle wins as the category grows.

6. @Backpack

→ No VC or team allocation at launch
→ BP stakers can potentially convert tokens into Backpack equity
→ The equity pool represents up to 20% of the company

BP already holds the EU stack. Japan and the U.S. are next.

7. @AerodromeFi

→ Main DEX/LP hub on Base
→ Coinbase putting DEX trading inside its app brings more retail flow toward Base apps
→ Majority of LP/volume/fees/voting activity sits on Aerodrome

Base is not going anywhere, and Aerodrome is an important centerpiece there.

8. @arcus_xyz

→ Big distribution edge with Robinhood's 20M+ users
→ Stock perps expand the market past BTC/ETH/SOL leverage
→ Pre-IPO + tokenized assets put it closer to a global trading venue

This is dYdX's giga-brain attempt to stand up to giants that stole its market share. A very smart move if you ask me.

9. @OndoPerps

→ The leader across tokenized Treasuries and tokenized stocks
→ Brings RWAs into a 24/7 trading format
→ Fits the demand for stocks/indices/RWAs onchain

If RWAs are the next big crypto category, and I'm confident they are, Ondo is already the venue for them.

10. @Raydium

→ Main Solana spot DEX (along with JUP)
→ Captures meme/launchpad/retail trading flow
→ RAY has a clearer link to DEX usage than most Solana tokens

Another bet on Solana's future performance and relevance.

tweets.text:
if your base port secured the whole trade it doesnt really reduce the risk. if meta drops hard it means the rest of your port is likely dropping hard and theres a broad sell off which would be the same time you'd have to come up with the 275k if you get assigned

your upside is capped a 64k no matter what

lets say a broad market sell off happens, meta falls to 300. you are still obligated to pay 550 - premium so 421. and then you have a paper loss of 60,500 after keeping the premium

its not really as risk free as you make it sound in this sense. long dated = more variables

tweets.text:
amigo, this is longer than my article

in asia, the best presentations were when i was up close with the community, walking through things step-by-step.  (see attached video)

covered bayc, otherside, apecoin - the apecoin content in particular is covered above 

a dialogue more than a lecture. i leave room for myself to be wrong about things and open to changing my pov. but if i'm right, it's a more potent format to build conviction in an audience.

no apechurch wasn't a part of that presentation. made that decision 2 days ago.  ruminated on it while flying back from asia as apes in shanghai expressed potential in the platform.  dug deeper into the app and saw that it was actually the best performing app in its class on any chain.  a 4M house placement seemed like an obvious move to make.  protocols should double down on winners.

apecoin is still the coin of the realm on apechain and otherside.  my argument here is that it's oversold.  there's still a case to be made for the upside beyond the HTF support level (part ii)

think longing bayc/mayc is perfectly fine.  but looking at the ratio, there's a 20:1 preference toward the nft vs the fungible.  like a good car engine, all the parts need to be a bit more balanced for the system to be at its best.  i think 20:1 is too lopsided.  better if we don't have an achilles heel in our eco.

yes there are dedicated teams across these 3 efforts.  but at times they can also become very interrelated so cross-functional roles across multiple areas are sometimes a better fit.

tweets.text:
🚨 Is sulphuric acid becoming the next big #uranium supply risk?

Cameco has just suspended operations at Cigar Lake due to sulphuric acid availability issues at Orano’s McClean Lake mill.

https://t.co/3op9zBMMpY

Now look at Kazakhstan — home to the world’s largest uranium producer, Kazatomprom.

🇷🇺 Russia has extended its ban on industrial sulphur exports through the end of 2026.

https://t.co/h1PL1G9jsd

🇰🇿 Kazakhstan has also suspended sulphur exports, effective June 27, with an exception for Russia.

https://t.co/NccsHUNCch

Why does this matter?

Kazatomprom’s massive ISR uranium operations depend heavily on sulphuric acid — and acid availability has already been a major constraint on production growth.

Its new 800,000 t/year sulphuric acid plant is still not confirmed online.

Cigar Lake just gave the uranium market another reminder of how critical sulphuric acid is.

Could Kazakhstan be the next supply risk the market is underestimating? 👀☢️

Can he tell us more about the recent news on assays showing higher grades than initially expected in ore sent to White Mesa? What kind of average grades are we talking about? What does it mean for the rest of the Sunday Mine complex? 
Are there any plans to resume trucking of ore to White Mesa? Are they preparing more trucks or licensed drivers? At what uranium price would they consider restart mining and trucking of ore?
What about their other projects? Any current plans for their potential ISR project (Hansen/Taylor)?
Financial situation? Cash position? How will they raise cash going forward?

tweets.text:
what is it about data centers that offends you? 

it's a quiet building that performs computation. it brings in massive amounts of revenue that can be used to offset property taxes. it doesn't have to be anywhere near residential areas. it's no more aesthetically offensive than a warehouse. it doesn't consume meaningful water; it does consume power - but are you against florida producing sufficient power to be an industrial powerhouse?

tweets.text:
I’m really excited about what’s going on with Digital Ray, which is my digital twin. I'm creating this so that everyone can have as much contact and as many conversations with me as they want—unlimited amounts, in the same sort of way that I do it. Eventually, it’s going to be a lot smarter than I am.

This is all part of the evolutionary process. It’s still a bit experimental, but it really is something. If you want to have unlimited conversations with me, go online and try it.

I also highly recommend taking the Principles You personality test. It’s online and only takes about 20 or 30 minutes. It will tell you all about your characteristics and inclinations.  Integrating your assessment with Digital Ray allows for highly personalized guidance in Coach Mode, tailored to your unique needs across life and work.

You can find a link here: https://t.co/daCo3GY70r

#DigitalRay #ArtificialIntelligence #PrinciplesYou #RayDalio

agent sandboxes run on purpose built bare metal, cheaper and faster! 

great new release from @nirvanalabsai - who knew four years spent optimizing high data throughput / egress for blockchain indexing would be so important for agents 🫡
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: 3/ Nirvana Agent Sandboxes run on NKS worker nodes: VMs sealed by a lightweight hypervisor. Each sandbox is a hardened, unprivileged container. Two layers of isolation, plus a dedicated cluster per customer. You never share a kernel with another company. https://t.co/oNvBzXYkNG
tweets.full_text:

Thank you to the @meetthedrapers team for editing and putting this together!

tweets.text:
I remember when at my previous company Doctrine we rewrote our frontend from AngularJS 1.7 to React. It was a huge project and a big decision. It took roughly six months and a team of talented software engineers. 

That’s why the Bun rewrite is so interesting to me. Bun was ~535k lines of Zig. The rewrite produced more than 1M lines of Rust diff! But... it took only 11 days. 

At peak they had around 64 agents running in parallel, across 4 worktrees, with 16 agents per workflow. It generated 6,500+ commits (wow!)

It was also a lot of work because they didn't ask "plz Code go from Zig to Rust" but they created a porting guide, a flow to analyze errors, separate adversarial agents to review diff to find mistakes etc. The human job was not to write the code but more designing and supervising the process aka "managing agents."

IMO it's a big deal. The world is full of important software stuck on old frameworks, old languages, old architectures and old security models because rewriting is too expensive and too risky. 

If this methodology becomes reliable, we can move software to safer languages, better runtimes, cleaner architectures and more secure defaults much faster. 

So much software run legacy COBOL and soon the transition to Rust will be easy.

At peak they had around 64 agents running in parallel, across 4 worktrees, with 16 agents per workflow. It generated 6,500+ commits (wow!)

tweets.text:
Microsoft just released TypeScript 7! It’s much faster.  Often 8x to 12x faster on full builds like VS Code went from 125s to ~10s (11x faster!)

There’s a real world where Python remains the language of ML with PyTorch, JAX etc, but TypeScript becomes the language of AI.

Most AI apps are not training models. They’re calling APIs, orchestrating tools, streaming tokens, managing state, auth, billing, workflows, agents, eval dashboards, and UX. Most companies are using TypeScript for that.

That world is getting much more TypeScript-shaped. Basically, Python trains the models and TypeScript ships the product.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Huge milestone for our team today: TypeScript 7 is now generally available--a native port that runs 10x faster. @typescript https://t.co/3DtJJqEZAl
